{{Quote|Beep boo weep. Twee twee.|3C-FD|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
'''3C-FD''' was a [[3C-series utility droid]] that operated during the [[Dark Wars]]. The droid was on the light [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' when it was coming back from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] carrying [[Meetra Surik]]—an ex–[[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] also known as the Jedi Exile—returning to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] after her [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]].

3C-FD helped the [[Maintenance droid/Legends|utility droid]] [[T3-M4]], who was on a mission to find the [[Gender/Legends|woman]], to repair the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to adequate flying condition after a disastrous escape from the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Together, the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were able to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] the ship close to the [[Peragus Mining Facility]]; 3C-FD, however, was destroyed by an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]] before the severely damaged ship and its unconscious passengers reached the docks of the facility.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Dee-deet tree-deet?|T3-M4 invites 3C-FD to join the repairs|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
[[File:3CFD1.jpg|thumb|left|260px|3C-FD on the hull of the ''Ebon Hawk'']]
3C-FD was a [[3C-series utility droid]] created by [[Duwani Mechanical Products/Legends|Duwani Mechanical Products]]<ref name="TNEGD" /> during the [[Time/Legends|time]] period of the [[Jedi Civil War]] and the [[Dark Wars]]. In [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="KotOR CG" /> 3C-FD was on board the light [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' when it escaped from the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-infiltrated [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|cruiser]] [[Harbinger (Hammerhead-class)|''Harbinger'']] by jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], after taking a severe amount of damage. The unconscious former [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]<ref name="kotor2" /> [[Meetra Surik]]<ref name="revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref>—known as the Jedi Exile, now returning to the Galactic Republic after her [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]], was brought on board from the ''Harbinger'' by the [[Maintenance droid/Legends|utility droid]] [[T3-M4]] and ex–[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kreia]], who were both attempting to find the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and bring her to safety. When the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] emerged from hyperspace near the [[Peragus asteroid field]] around the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Peragus II]], it was drifting in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] without functioning
[[Engine/Legends|engines]].<ref name="kotor2" />

The ''Ebon Hawk'' seemed lifeless, except for T3-M4, who began his attempt to fix the ship to flying condition. Within the cargo hold, he found a damaged 3C-FD and repaired the [[Droid/Legends|droid]], inviting it to accompany him. The duo gathered components from a [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] and parts that were scattered around the ship, and they used these for the required repairs. After repairing the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], they restored power to the port engine. The starboard engine remained inoperative, but one engine was enough to make the ship spaceworthy, and T3-M4, with 3C-FD, was able to pilot the ''Ebon Hawk'' into the [[Peragus Mining Facility]].<ref name="kotor2" />

Prior to the ''Ebon Hawk''{{'s}} arrival on Peragus [[Space station/Legends|Station]], 3C-FD was [[Blaster/Legends|shot]] several times and irreparably damaged by a stowaway [[HK-50 series assassin droid]] hiding aboard the ''Ebon Hawk''. The Peragus officials placed the droid's remains into a [[quarantine]] locker, and were later found in there by T3-M4. Soon after these [[Skirmish at Peragus II|events]], the facility was destroyed and 3C-FD with it.<ref name="kotor2" />

==Characteristics==
3C-FD was a typical Duwani-made utility droid. It had four wheeled legs, two of which were long and jointed toward the front. Including the droid's flat rotating head, 3C-FD was a bit under one [[Meter/Legends|meter]] high. The droid's equipment included a multi-functional [[Limb/Legends|arm]], an auditory and sonar [[scanner]], a radar [[Eye/Legends|eye]], and visual [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] capable handling all base colors.<ref name="TNEGD" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Dwoo bwooo.|3C-FD refuses to join the player's party|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
[[File:AlasPoor3C-K2.png|thumb|left|3C-FD, after being damaged by an HK-50 series assassin droid]]
3C-FD was first introduced in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] developed by [[Obsidian Entertainment]] that was first released in [[December]] [[2004]]. The droid initially appears as a playable character in an option tutorial section called "Prelude" where the player learns how to interact with the world in the game while repairing the ''Ebon Hawk'' with T3-M4, and later in the Peragus Mining Station.<ref name="kotor2" />

There are at least two options for the player to use 3C-FD in Peragus: it can be issued a [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destruct code]] by T3-M4 in order to blast open the door to the locker from the inside, allowing T3 access to the ship's other gear. Alternatively, the door can be blasted open from the outside and 3C-FD merely abandoned in its broken state. Sometimes, when returning into the quarantine area, the game's program forgets to render 3C-FD as destroyed due to a scripting error. Therefore, the droid looks around, bounces, and beeps. When the player clicks on the droid, the text states that 3C-FD is destroyed beyond repair.<ref name="kotor2" />

''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', a [[2006]] [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|Essential Guide]] written by [[Daniel Wallace]], has an entry for 3C-FD, containing its technical data and a summary of the events that led to its destruction.<ref name="TNEGD" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1st}}
